metaBuilds.title: Crit Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 26 — about 1,313 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 2% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Crit Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 877 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +48% more 10s rotation damage: try Viper over Lightning Hawk (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Crit DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 413 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +34% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Lightning Hawk (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Sparrow ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 37% of your damage.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 37% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Drongo deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 37% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 37% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 35% of its bite; you give up 37% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Martial Law” ⇒ ability-burst; the sim models this augment — field midlane 48.8% over 21,858 games.

flags: crests and consumables not yet in the build model; augments with tractable mechanics are simulated, the rest are listed unmodeled
